title: rubus gracilis var. parvulus
intro: Rubus gracilis var. parvulus is a lesser-known variety of the Rubus gracilis species, commonly referred to as the dwarf or small-leaf Himalayan raspberry, which is a type of wild raspberry native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus gracilis var. parvulus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Rubus gracilis var. parvulus can be prepared for medicinal use in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions.
